romania.title: A fost câștigat premiul de peste 4 milioane de lei la tragerea Noroc a Loteriei Române. Unde s-a jucat biletul norocos
romania.summary: Premiul de categoria I în valoare de 4,074 milioane de lei la tragerea Noroc de duminică a fost câştigat, a anunţat, luni, Loteria Română.

romania.title: 😂 La presiunea lui Trump, FIFA a decis că SUA va avea poartă de hochei în meciul cu Belgia
romania.summary: Donald Trump nu a fost mulțumit doar cu anularea suspendării pentru jucătorul american Folarin Balogun, așa că a revenit cu încă o cerere către președintele FIFA, Gianni Infantino: în meciul din optimi cu Belgia, poarta americanilor să fie de dimensiunile uneia de hochei. Așa cum era de așteptat, Gianni Infantino a zis „Da, bineînțeles!” încă […] The post La presiunea lui Trump, FIFA a decis că SUA va avea poartă de hochei în meciul cu Belgia appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: Activele fondurilor de pensii private au ajuns la 227,5 miliarde de lei. Cum sunt investiți banii românilor din Pilonul II
romania.summary: Fondurile de pensii private obligatorii (Pilonul II) aveau active în valoare de 227,5 miliarde de lei la finalul lunii mai 2026, în creștere cu 37% față de aceeași perioadă a anului trecut, potrivit datelor Autorității de Supraveghere Financiară (ASF). În același timp, activele fondurilor de pensii facultative (Pilonul III) au ajuns la 8,4 miliarde de lei, înregistrând un avans de 38% comparativ cu luna mai 2025.

romania.title: Peru a stabilit un record mondial Guinness cu cel mai mare curs de dans salsa din lume
romania.summary: Sute de cupluri s-au adunat la cel mai mare curs de salsa din lume pe 4 iulie 2026, la Lima, în Peru. Peste 2.000 de cupluri au participat la un curs de salsa de amploare organizat la Lima, stabilind astfel recordul mondial Guinness pentru cel mai mare curs de salsa din lume, scrie Reuters.

romania.title: 10 IMM-uri certificate B Corp în România. Impactul certificării asupra companiilor

romania.title: CM 2026. Neymar și-a anunțat retragerea de la Naționala Braziliei, după eliminarea în optimile de finală ale Cupei Mondiale
romania.summary: Veteranul Neymar și-a anunțat retragerea din echipa națională după eliminarea Braziliei în optimile de finală ale Cupei Mondiale. „Am încercat, chiar am încercat. Acum s-a terminat”, a declarat jucătorul în vârstă de 34 de ani pentru postul de televiziune Globo după înfrângerea cu 1-2 împotriva Norvegiei. „Am început aici, am terminat aici”, a adăugat acesta, potrivit focus.de.

romania.title: Trafic rutier închis luni pe mai multe tronsoane ale DN 7C – Transfăgărăşan. Recomandările Poliției
romania.summary: Traficul rutier se va închide, luni, 6 iulie, pe mai multe tronsoane de drum ale DN 7C – Transfăgărăşan pentru desfăşurarea a două evenimente, unul fiind sportiv iar celălalt, o şedinţă foto privată, cu autoturisme. Poliţiştii rutieri le recomandă şoferilor să evite deplasarea pe acest drum şi să respecte semnalizările de circulaţie montate temporar în zonele respective.

Brașovul a devenit, pentru câteva ore, … Read More The post Brașovul a devenit capitala tinerilor antreprenori din România la Arena Urșilor Junior 2026 appeared first on Stiri pozitive .

medical.title: New tumor map identifies high-risk B-cell lymphoma standard therapy may miss
medical.summary: Researchers led by Universitätsmedizin Frankfurt and Goethe University Frankfurt have identified how particularly aggressive forms of lymphoma can be recognized. By combining genetic and proteomic analyses, the scientists identified biological characteristics of tumors, particularly in high-risk patients for whom standard therapy offers little chance of cure. In the future, such patients could receive alternative, more effective therapies directly. In addition, experimental laboratory research provided initial clues to potential therapeutic targets. The study is published in Cancer Cell.

medical.title: Early dog exposure may protect babies from infections through home microbes
medical.summary: Children who have been in contact with dogs in early childhood are, on average, healthier and require fewer courses of antibiotics than children without such contact. The protective association is explained, at least in part, by the spread of dog-associated microbes in the home.

medical.title: Researcher investigates enthusiasm as a distinct psychological emotion
medical.summary: Enthusiasm receives little attention in psychology. PhD researcher Rijn Vogelaar aims to change that. His doctoral dissertation research shows that enthusiasm is a distinct emotion that not only gives people energy, but also motivates them to take action and connect with others.

medical.title: Patients in Hawai'i spent $230M+ on out-of-state cancer care in 2021–2023, study finds
medical.summary: A new study led by researchers at the University of Hawaiʻi Cancer Center found that at least $230 million was spent on cancer care delivered outside Hawaiʻi between 2021 and 2023, highlighting the significant financial impact of geographic isolation on patients and the health care system.

medical.title: Biological aortic valves linked to better pregnancy outcomes, but more reoperations
medical.summary: In cases of serious aortic valve disease, choosing a new heart valve is particularly difficult for young women who wish to become pregnant. A new Swedish registry study from Karolinska Institutet shows that a biological aortic valve provides good pregnancy and birth outcomes, even though the need for reoperation is more common than with a mechanical valve. The study is published in the JACC.

medical.title: Digital diabetes tool shown to improve patient care and cut health care costs
medical.summary: A new study by University of Manchester researchers has linked a digital platform helping people manage type 2 diabetes to better blood sugar control, improved health and potential long-term NHS cost savings. MyWay Diabetes, an NHS-supported online platform and app—which lets users view their diabetes health records, track results and access simple education courses and advice—was rolled out across Greater Manchester during the COVID-19 pandemic.

medical.title: Nursing leaders call on profession to amplify its impact ahead of U.S. semiquincentennial
medical.summary: As the United States prepares to mark its 250th anniversary, Penn Nursing leaders are issuing a joint call to action for the nursing profession. In separate commentaries published in the July 2026 issue of the American Journal of Nursing (AJN), Antonia M. Villarruel, PhD, RN, FAAN, the Margaret Bond Simon Dean of Nursing, and J. Margo Brooks Carthon, PhD, RN, FAAN, director of the Barbara Bates Center for the Study of the History of Nursing, urge nurses to reclaim their foundational role in America's history and use that legacy to advocate for social justice and professional equity today.

medical.title: Analysis indicates Latinos in U.S. are indispensable to nation's prosperity, health, future
medical.summary: The conventional narrative that Latinos are taking more from the United States than they contribute is not just wrong—it is dangerous. In a new "Medicine and Society" analysis published in the New England Journal of Medicine, researchers from the Center for Latino Adolescent and Family Health (CLAFH) at John Hopkins University School of Nursing report that Latinos are among the nation's most important contributors to economic growth, workforce participation, and population health.

medical.title: A surprising discovery reveals the kidney has a secret backup system
medical.summary: Scientists at Mayo Clinic have uncovered a surprising new way the kidneys conserve water, revealing a hidden backup system that works independently of the hormone long believed to control the process. The discovery emerged when researchers testing an old drug called probenecid expected it to worsen polycystic kidney disease (PKD) but found it actually slowed cyst growth instead.

science.title: Bioinspired strategy creates complex 3D curved structures via programmed shrinkage
science.summary: The shape of biological structures, ranging from flower petals to the limbs or organs of animals, is often naturally best suited for performing specific functions. Biological structures also often present curved surfaces with specific functional advantages, such as facilitating the drainage of water, increasing a structure's strength or aerodynamic efficiency, or supporting heavy loads.

science.title: Phylogenomics reveals angel insects' ancestry, resolving century‑old 'Zoraptera problem'
science.summary: Zoraptera, also known as angel insects or ground lice, are tiny termite-like insects generally found underneath bark or in decaying wood. The Zoraptera group includes a few dozen known insect species that closely resemble each other.

science.title: Entrepreneurs more likely to be based in left wing areas
science.summary: Research from the University of St. Andrews is challenging conventional assumptions about the relationship between politics and entrepreneurship. The study explored whether the political environment in which people live influences their likelihood of starting a business, and its findings reveal that areas with predominantly left-leaning political views have the highest rates of business creation, while areas with stronger right-wing political affiliations record the lowest.

science.title: Scholars examine how faculty define rigor in online college courses
science.summary: A new article by scholars Jennifer L. James, Ph.D., Karen Myers, DNP, and Olivia Miller, M.A., in the Journal of Educators Online, titled "Studying Faculty Perceptions of Rigor in Online College Courses: Compromising or Accommodating?" examines how faculty perceive academic rigor when teaching, grading and managing online courses for nontraditional students.

science.title: Study reveals social enterprise well-being pressures
science.summary: Social enterprises help communities thrive, but founder well-being is critical to sustaining their impact, UC research finds. Social enterprises create jobs, encourage ethical consumption, and address social and environmental challenges.

science.title: Did Israel's 'basket' initiative lower grocery bills?
science.summary: Researchers from the Coller School of Management at Tel Aviv University examined the Ministry of Economy's flagship "Israel's Basket" initiative and found that although it substantially reduced the prices of the 100 products included in the program, these reductions were accompanied by price increases across many other product categories. According to the researchers, the overall impact on consumers' grocery bills may therefore have been considerably smaller than expected.

science.title: Researchers use AI to evaluate a systematic framework to describe molecular order in liquid water
science.summary: Water is the most abundant liquid on Earth's surface, and it is highly anomalous compared with other liquids because it expands upon freezing. The anomalies in water have been linked to how its microscopic structure changes with temperature and pressure. However, there is no systematic scheme for characterizing these structural changes.

science.title: Neutral lipids enable precision control over supramolecular polymerization
science.summary: The formation of supramolecular polymers within living cells is an emerging strategy for regulating cellular functions, and lipid droplets (LDs) are promising environments for such processes. LDs are cellular organelles composed mainly of neutral lipids, such as triacylglycerols and cholesterol esters, and are deeply involved in cellular functions. However, the self-assembly of supramolecular polymers in neutral lipid-rich environments such as LDs is not well understood.

science.title: Streetlights are trapping thousands of pill bugs in giant “death spirals”
science.summary: Researchers discovered that artificial streetlights can trap thousands of woodlice in mesmerizing circular "death spirals" never before seen in the wild. The surprising finding suggests that light pollution may be unintentionally altering the behavior of even the smallest ground-dwelling animals.

science.title: 'Major' damage as super typhoon hits US islands
science.summary: A "super typhoon" with the force of a Category 5 hurricane tore through the U.S. Pacific territories of the Northern Marianas and Guam on Monday, with authorities saying they had received reports of "major" damage on the small island of Rota.

science.title: China's space probe reaches asteroid after 1-billion-kilometer chase for first sample return
science.summary: China's Tianwen-2 space probe, which is set to bring back samples from an asteroid for research, has reached its target after traveling 1 billion kilometers (620 million miles) over more than a year, the Chinese space agency said Monday.

environment.title: Bornean ferret badger only lives in Borneo. Could it be a conservation symbol?
environment.summary: The Bornean ferret badger is a small carnivore with the slinky body of a ferret and a face mask like a badger. A new study confirms that it lives only in the mountains of Sabah, a Malaysian state on the island of Borneo. Ferret badgers are nocturnal carnivores, widespread across Southeast Asia, but the Bornean […]

environment.title: Mozambique completes first white rhino breeding population in decades
environment.summary: On June 6, nine female white rhinos arrived in Mozambique’s Zinave National Park following a two-day translocation. Their arrival marks the culmination of nearly 10 years of rhino reintroduction efforts in the park, aimed at rebuilding a viable breeding population of the mammals in Zinave after decades of local extinction. The white rhinos (Ceratotherium simum) […]

environment.title: ‘Flamingo Revolution’ aims to stop Kushner-backed resort on protected Albanian delta
environment.summary: VJOSA-NARTA, Albania — In late April, heavy machinery began moving into the Pishë Poro-Narta protected landscape on Albania’s Adriatic coast without permits or public notice. Bulldozers and excavators felled coastal pine trees, flattened sand dunes, and cut new roads through previously untouched habitat. Then, barbed wire fences went up along the shoreline. The incursion was […]

environment.title: The secret underground system keeping the Grand Canyon alive
environment.summary: Scientists are venturing into the Grand Canyon’s hidden cave networks to solve a mystery: how snowmelt travels underground to supply the park’s vital springs. Their discoveries could help protect the canyon’s water from drought, contamination, and other growing threats.

environment.title: DNA solves 250-year-old mystery of the Seychelles’ lost crocodiles
environment.summary: Scientists have solved the mystery of the Seychelles’ vanished crocodiles using DNA from historic museum specimens. The reptiles were not a unique species after all, but an isolated population of saltwater crocodiles that likely drifted thousands of kilometers across the Indian Ocean.

environment.title: Scottish wrens may be evolving into new species through island gigantism
environment.summary: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation. Scientists studying four isolated populations of British Wrens discovered that some island birds have grown astonishingly large — with the biggest St Kilda Wrens weighing more than twice as much as the smallest mainland birds. The research suggests these wrens are evolving independently, developing unique songs, appearances, and genetics that may eventually turn them into entirely new species.

environment.title: A New York cemetery was hiding 5.5 million bees underground
environment.summary: A casual walk through an Ithaca cemetery led to the discovery of a gigantic hidden bee population — roughly 5.5 million ground-nesting bees packed beneath the soil. Scientists believe it may be one of the largest bee aggregations ever documented and say the insects are crucial pollinators for apple orchards and other crops. The bees have likely lived there for more than 100 years, thriving in the cemetery’s undisturbed sandy soil.

environment.title: Earth’s orbital wobble triggered rapid climate chaos during the dinosaur age
environment.summary: New research suggests Earth’s climate can swing wildly on surprisingly short timescales — even during hot, ice-free greenhouse periods. By studying ancient sediments from the Late Cretaceous, scientists uncovered repeating climate shifts tied to tiny changes in Earth’s orbital wobble. These cycles may have repeatedly pushed the planet between humid and arid states every few thousand years.

mix_items.title: Jazz in the Park Competition anunță noua generație de jazz european: 12 trupe din 7 țări europene urcă în septembrie pe scena competiției
mix_items.summary: Jazz in the Park Competition 2026 anunță cele 12 proiecte finaliste care vor urca pe scena competiției între 18 și 20 septembrie, în Parcul Central din Cluj-Napoca. Din 306 formații… Articolul Jazz in the Park Competition anunță noua generație de jazz european: 12 trupe din 7 țări europene urcă în septembrie pe scena competiției apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

mix_items.title: 😂 La presiunea lui Trump, FIFA a decis că SUA va avea poartă de hochei în meciul cu Belgia
mix_items.summary: Donald Trump nu a fost mulțumit doar cu anularea suspendării pentru jucătorul american Folarin Balogun, așa că a revenit cu încă o cerere către președintele FIFA, Gianni Infantino: în meciul din optimi cu Belgia, poarta americanilor să fie de dimensiunile uneia de hochei. Așa cum era de așteptat, Gianni Infantino a zis „Da, bineînțeles!” încă […] The post La presiunea lui Trump, FIFA a decis că SUA va avea poartă de hochei în meciul cu Belgia appeared first on Times New Roman .

mix_items.title: Delia, Eduard Novak și Towns Up Forum completează programul Zilele Nordului 2026
mix_items.summary: Asociația Nord anunță al doilea val de noutăți și evenimente pentru cea de-a XIII-a ediție a festivalului Zilele Nordului, care va transforma orașul Darabani în epicentrul cultural al regiunii celor… Articolul Delia, Eduard Novak și Towns Up Forum completează programul Zilele Nordului 2026 apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

mix_items.summary: Mai plutește deasupra Sibiului norul de aplauze de la Festival. Felicităm spectatorii de pe Scena Digitală pentru că s-a triplat numărul lor la FITS 2026! De aceea, propunem întâlniri cu… Articolul Scena Digitală propune în luna iulie două întâlniri cu emoția: Broadway și o poveste despre iubire, familie și acceptare apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

mix_items.title: „Atlasul Universului”, o poveste premiată despre aventura unui copil de 10 ani, își lansează posterul și trailerul în România
mix_items.summary: „Atlasul Universului”, filmul pentru copii și familie regizat de Paul Negoescu, primit cu entuziasm în cadrul Festivalului Internațional de Film Transilvania, ediția 25, după participările și premiile în festivaluri internaționale,… Articolul „Atlasul Universului”, o poveste premiată despre aventura unui copil de 10 ani, își lansează posterul și trailerul în România apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

mix_items.title: CM 2026. Neymar și-a anunțat retragerea de la Naționala Braziliei, după eliminarea în optimile de finală ale Cupei Mondiale
mix_items.summary: Veteranul Neymar și-a anunțat retragerea din echipa națională după eliminarea Braziliei în optimile de finală ale Cupei Mondiale. „Am încercat, chiar am încercat. Acum s-a terminat”, a declarat jucătorul în vârstă de 34 de ani pentru postul de televiziune Globo după înfrângerea cu 1-2 împotriva Norvegiei. „Am început aici, am terminat aici”, a adăugat acesta, potrivit focus.de.

mix_items.title: Trafic rutier închis luni pe mai multe tronsoane ale DN 7C – Transfăgărăşan. Recomandările Poliției
mix_items.summary: Traficul rutier se va închide, luni, 6 iulie, pe mai multe tronsoane de drum ale DN 7C – Transfăgărăşan pentru desfăşurarea a două evenimente, unul fiind sportiv iar celălalt, o şedinţă foto privată, cu autoturisme. Poliţiştii rutieri le recomandă şoferilor să evite deplasarea pe acest drum şi să respecte semnalizările de circulaţie montate temporar în zonele respective.

mix_items.title: Bioinspired strategy creates complex 3D curved structures via programmed shrinkage
mix_items.summary: The shape of biological structures, ranging from flower petals to the limbs or organs of animals, is often naturally best suited for performing specific functions. Biological structures also often present curved surfaces with specific functional advantages, such as facilitating the drainage of water, increasing a structure's strength or aerodynamic efficiency, or supporting heavy loads.

mix_items.title: Evaluarea Națională 2026: Tranziția către liceu, punctul critic al abandonului școlar. World Vision România propune un plan de intervenție
mix_items.summary: București, 1 iulie. Rezultatele Evaluării Naționale 2026 reconfirmă decalajele profunde dintre copiii din mediul rural și cei din mediul urban... Articolul Evaluarea Națională 2026: Tranziția către liceu, punctul critic al abandonului școlar. World Vision România propune un plan de intervenție apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Startup-urile și IMM-urile recrutează mai ușor decât corporațiile. Ce observă Recrutopia pe piața muncii
mix_items.summary: Candidații care vor să-și schimbe locul de muncă au șanse mai mari să găsească un rol într-un startup sau într-un IMM decât într-o corporație, unde competiția este mai mare, procesele de recrutare...

mix_items.title: Phylogenomics reveals angel insects' ancestry, resolving century‑old 'Zoraptera problem'
mix_items.summary: Zoraptera, also known as angel insects or ground lice, are tiny termite-like insects generally found underneath bark or in decaying wood. The Zoraptera group includes a few dozen known insect species that closely resemble each other.

mix_items.title: Cum se transformă operatorii mobili. Orange România, de la furnizor de rețea la furnizori de servicii digitale
mix_items.summary: Orange România și-a dezvăluit strategia pentru următorii ani în România, dar și la nivel global, numită Trust the Future. Aceasta se concentrează pe dezvoltarea de rețele 5G+, unde România încă...

mix_items.title: Oana Opriș - Genuin | ”Familia stă mereu înaintea banilor”
mix_items.summary: Oana Opriș, alături de soțul ei și cumnații lor au creat o afacere care readuce inul în casele românilor. Genuin este brand-ul din Brașov sub care afacerea de familie produce așternuturi, perdele...

mix_items.title: Entrepreneurs more likely to be based in left wing areas
mix_items.summary: Research from the University of St. Andrews is challenging conventional assumptions about the relationship between politics and entrepreneurship. The study explored whether the political environment in which people live influences their likelihood of starting a business, and its findings reveal that areas with predominantly left-leaning political views have the highest rates of business creation, while areas with stronger right-wing political affiliations record the lowest.

mix_items.title: Părinte Social
mix_items.summary: Părinte Social (Mamă SOS) – București și Cisnădie, Sibiu Transformă un loc de muncă într-o misiune de viață La SOS Satele Copiilor România, credem că fiecare copil are dreptul să... The post Părinte Social appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.summary: Locație: Bacău | Nivel carieră: Experiență de minimum 1 an Candidatul Ideal: Ești tu vocea de care au nevoie? Un copil are nevoie de iubire, siguranță și încredere pentru a... The post Psiholog appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.title: Scholars examine how faculty define rigor in online college courses
mix_items.summary: A new article by scholars Jennifer L. James, Ph.D., Karen Myers, DNP, and Olivia Miller, M.A., in the Journal of Educators Online, titled "Studying Faculty Perceptions of Rigor in Online College Courses: Compromising or Accommodating?" examines how faculty perceive academic rigor when teaching, grading and managing online courses for nontraditional students.

The post Asistent Social appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.title: Study reveals social enterprise well-being pressures
mix_items.summary: Social enterprises help communities thrive, but founder well-being is critical to sustaining their impact, UC research finds. Social enterprises create jobs, encourage ethical consumption, and address social and environmental challenges.

mix_items.title: Pentru Fundația Motivation, duminica trecută a fost despre fapte bune și, mai ales, despre o echipă unită în jurul unui scop dedicat binelui.
mix_items.summary: #TeamMotivation a alergat la Semimaraton Galați ca să schimbe în bine viețile copiilor și adulților cu dizabilități care au nevoie de sprijin în încercarea lor de a-și recăpăta independența. Evenimentul organizat de Fundația Comunitară Galați a adunat ambasadori a zeci de cauze pentru care alergătorii participanți au făcut tot posibilul să ajungă la finish. Pe lângă emoțiile și încurajările care au condimentat cursele din cadrul maratonului, pentru Fundația Motivation evenimentul a arătat așa: 14 utilizatori de scaun rulant... The post Pentru Fundația Motivation, duminica trecută a fost despre fapte bune și, mai ales, despre o echipă unită în jurul unui scop dedicat binelui. appeared first on Fundatia Motivation Romania .

mix_items.title: Brașovul a devenit capitala tinerilor antreprenori din România la Arena Urșilor Junior 2026
mix_items.summary: Zbor Hub Brașov a găzduit finala națională Arena Urșilor Junior 2026, o competiție dedicată liceenilor cu idei de afaceri curajoase și planuri antreprenoriale inovatoare. Brașovul a devenit, pentru câteva ore, … Read More The post Brașovul a devenit capitala tinerilor antreprenori din România la Arena Urșilor Junior 2026 appeared first on Stiri pozitive .

mix_items.title: Did Israel's 'basket' initiative lower grocery bills?
mix_items.summary: Researchers from the Coller School of Management at Tel Aviv University examined the Ministry of Economy's flagship "Israel's Basket" initiative and found that although it substantially reduced the prices of the 100 products included in the program, these reductions were accompanied by price increases across many other product categories. According to the researchers, the overall impact on consumers' grocery bills may therefore have been considerably smaller than expected.

mix_items.title: Researchers use AI to evaluate a systematic framework to describe molecular order in liquid water
mix_items.summary: Water is the most abundant liquid on Earth's surface, and it is highly anomalous compared with other liquids because it expands upon freezing. The anomalies in water have been linked to how its microscopic structure changes with temperature and pressure. However, there is no systematic scheme for characterizing these structural changes.

mix_items.title: Neutral lipids enable precision control over supramolecular polymerization
mix_items.summary: The formation of supramolecular polymers within living cells is an emerging strategy for regulating cellular functions, and lipid droplets (LDs) are promising environments for such processes. LDs are cellular organelles composed mainly of neutral lipids, such as triacylglycerols and cholesterol esters, and are deeply involved in cellular functions. However, the self-assembly of supramolecular polymers in neutral lipid-rich environments such as LDs is not well understood.

mix_items.title: Streetlights are trapping thousands of pill bugs in giant “death spirals”
mix_items.summary: Researchers discovered that artificial streetlights can trap thousands of woodlice in mesmerizing circular "death spirals" never before seen in the wild. The surprising finding suggests that light pollution may be unintentionally altering the behavior of even the smallest ground-dwelling animals.

mix_items.title: 'Major' damage as super typhoon hits US islands
mix_items.summary: A "super typhoon" with the force of a Category 5 hurricane tore through the U.S. Pacific territories of the Northern Marianas and Guam on Monday, with authorities saying they had received reports of "major" damage on the small island of Rota.

mix_items.title: China's space probe reaches asteroid after 1-billion-kilometer chase for first sample return
mix_items.summary: China's Tianwen-2 space probe, which is set to bring back samples from an asteroid for research, has reached its target after traveling 1 billion kilometers (620 million miles) over more than a year, the Chinese space agency said Monday.

mix_items.title: New tumor map identifies high-risk B-cell lymphoma standard therapy may miss
mix_items.summary: Researchers led by Universitätsmedizin Frankfurt and Goethe University Frankfurt have identified how particularly aggressive forms of lymphoma can be recognized. By combining genetic and proteomic analyses, the scientists identified biological characteristics of tumors, particularly in high-risk patients for whom standard therapy offers little chance of cure. In the future, such patients could receive alternative, more effective therapies directly. In addition, experimental laboratory research provided initial clues to potential therapeutic targets. The study is published in Cancer Cell.

mix_items.title: Early dog exposure may protect babies from infections through home microbes
mix_items.summary: Children who have been in contact with dogs in early childhood are, on average, healthier and require fewer courses of antibiotics than children without such contact. The protective association is explained, at least in part, by the spread of dog-associated microbes in the home.

mix_items.title: Researcher investigates enthusiasm as a distinct psychological emotion
mix_items.summary: Enthusiasm receives little attention in psychology. PhD researcher Rijn Vogelaar aims to change that. His doctoral dissertation research shows that enthusiasm is a distinct emotion that not only gives people energy, but also motivates them to take action and connect with others.

mix_items.title: Patients in Hawai'i spent $230M+ on out-of-state cancer care in 2021–2023, study finds
mix_items.summary: A new study led by researchers at the University of Hawaiʻi Cancer Center found that at least $230 million was spent on cancer care delivered outside Hawaiʻi between 2021 and 2023, highlighting the significant financial impact of geographic isolation on patients and the health care system.

mix_items.title: Biological aortic valves linked to better pregnancy outcomes, but more reoperations
mix_items.summary: In cases of serious aortic valve disease, choosing a new heart valve is particularly difficult for young women who wish to become pregnant. A new Swedish registry study from Karolinska Institutet shows that a biological aortic valve provides good pregnancy and birth outcomes, even though the need for reoperation is more common than with a mechanical valve. The study is published in the JACC.

mix_items.title: Digital diabetes tool shown to improve patient care and cut health care costs
mix_items.summary: A new study by University of Manchester researchers has linked a digital platform helping people manage type 2 diabetes to better blood sugar control, improved health and potential long-term NHS cost savings. MyWay Diabetes, an NHS-supported online platform and app—which lets users view their diabetes health records, track results and access simple education courses and advice—was rolled out across Greater Manchester during the COVID-19 pandemic.

mix_items.title: Nursing leaders call on profession to amplify its impact ahead of U.S. semiquincentennial
mix_items.summary: As the United States prepares to mark its 250th anniversary, Penn Nursing leaders are issuing a joint call to action for the nursing profession. In separate commentaries published in the July 2026 issue of the American Journal of Nursing (AJN), Antonia M. Villarruel, PhD, RN, FAAN, the Margaret Bond Simon Dean of Nursing, and J. Margo Brooks Carthon, PhD, RN, FAAN, director of the Barbara Bates Center for the Study of the History of Nursing, urge nurses to reclaim their foundational role in America's history and use that legacy to advocate for social justice and professional equity today.

mix_items.title: Analysis indicates Latinos in U.S. are indispensable to nation's prosperity, health, future
mix_items.summary: The conventional narrative that Latinos are taking more from the United States than they contribute is not just wrong—it is dangerous. In a new "Medicine and Society" analysis published in the New England Journal of Medicine, researchers from the Center for Latino Adolescent and Family Health (CLAFH) at John Hopkins University School of Nursing report that Latinos are among the nation's most important contributors to economic growth, workforce participation, and population health.

mix_items.title: Bornean ferret badger only lives in Borneo. Could it be a conservation symbol?
mix_items.summary: The Bornean ferret badger is a small carnivore with the slinky body of a ferret and a face mask like a badger. A new study confirms that it lives only in the mountains of Sabah, a Malaysian state on the island of Borneo. Ferret badgers are nocturnal carnivores, widespread across Southeast Asia, but the Bornean […]

mix_items.title: Mozambique completes first white rhino breeding population in decades
mix_items.summary: On June 6, nine female white rhinos arrived in Mozambique’s Zinave National Park following a two-day translocation. Their arrival marks the culmination of nearly 10 years of rhino reintroduction efforts in the park, aimed at rebuilding a viable breeding population of the mammals in Zinave after decades of local extinction. The white rhinos (Ceratotherium simum) […]

mix_items.title: ‘Flamingo Revolution’ aims to stop Kushner-backed resort on protected Albanian delta
mix_items.summary: VJOSA-NARTA, Albania — In late April, heavy machinery began moving into the Pishë Poro-Narta protected landscape on Albania’s Adriatic coast without permits or public notice. Bulldozers and excavators felled coastal pine trees, flattened sand dunes, and cut new roads through previously untouched habitat. Then, barbed wire fences went up along the shoreline. The incursion was […]

mix_items.title: A surprising discovery reveals the kidney has a secret backup system
mix_items.summary: Scientists at Mayo Clinic have uncovered a surprising new way the kidneys conserve water, revealing a hidden backup system that works independently of the hormone long believed to control the process. The discovery emerged when researchers testing an old drug called probenecid expected it to worsen polycystic kidney disease (PKD) but found it actually slowed cyst growth instead.

mix_items.title: The secret underground system keeping the Grand Canyon alive
mix_items.summary: Scientists are venturing into the Grand Canyon’s hidden cave networks to solve a mystery: how snowmelt travels underground to supply the park’s vital springs. Their discoveries could help protect the canyon’s water from drought, contamination, and other growing threats.

mix_items.title: DNA solves 250-year-old mystery of the Seychelles’ lost crocodiles
mix_items.summary: Scientists have solved the mystery of the Seychelles’ vanished crocodiles using DNA from historic museum specimens. The reptiles were not a unique species after all, but an isolated population of saltwater crocodiles that likely drifted thousands of kilometers across the Indian Ocean.

mix_items.title: Scottish wrens may be evolving into new species through island gigantism
mix_items.summary: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation. Scientists studying four isolated populations of British Wrens discovered that some island birds have grown astonishingly large — with the biggest St Kilda Wrens weighing more than twice as much as the smallest mainland birds. The research suggests these wrens are evolving independently, developing unique songs, appearances, and genetics that may eventually turn them into entirely new species.

mix_items.title: A New York cemetery was hiding 5.5 million bees underground
mix_items.summary: A casual walk through an Ithaca cemetery led to the discovery of a gigantic hidden bee population — roughly 5.5 million ground-nesting bees packed beneath the soil. Scientists believe it may be one of the largest bee aggregations ever documented and say the insects are crucial pollinators for apple orchards and other crops. The bees have likely lived there for more than 100 years, thriving in the cemetery’s undisturbed sandy soil.

mix_items.title: Earth’s orbital wobble triggered rapid climate chaos during the dinosaur age
mix_items.summary: New research suggests Earth’s climate can swing wildly on surprisingly short timescales — even during hot, ice-free greenhouse periods. By studying ancient sediments from the Late Cretaceous, scientists uncovered repeating climate shifts tied to tiny changes in Earth’s orbital wobble. These cycles may have repeatedly pushed the planet between humid and arid states every few thousand years.

joke_ro.text: Ce ai rezolvat azi? - Toata cafeaua.

satire_ro.title: Peştele iepure e mic copil! Plajele româneşti, invadate de peştele boxă, zis şi jebeleul de mare
